sequentialTasks.task: Implement Screen scroll operations in vt/screen.go
sequentialTasks.description: Add methods to Screen: ScrollUp(n int), ScrollDown(n int), LineFeed(), ReverseIndex(). These replace scrollUp/scrollDown/lineFeed/reverseIndex from vterm.go. ScrollUp shifts rows upward within the scroll region [ScrollTop..ScrollBot], filling vacated bottom rows with CurrentAttr-colored blank lines (makeAttrLine pattern). ScrollDown shifts rows downward. LineFeed moves cursor down, scrolling if at bottom of scroll region. ReverseIndex moves cursor up, scrolling down if at top of scroll region. Use 1-indexed scroll region internally (0 = default = full screen).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test in vt/screen_test.go: ScrollUp(1) on a 5-row screen moves row 1 content to row 0, row 4 becomes blank. ScrollDown(1) does the reverse. LineFeed at bottom of scroll region triggers ScrollUp. ReverseIndex at top of scroll region triggers ScrollDown. Operations respect non-default scroll regions (e.g., ScrollTop=2, ScrollBot=4 on a 5-row screen). Blank lines carry current Attr.

sequentialTasks.task: Define ANSI parser state machine types in vt/parser.go
sequentialTasks.description: Create internal/termmux/vt/parser.go. Define `type State uint8` with constants: StateGround=0, StateEscape=1, StateCSI=2, StateOSC=3, StateDCS=4. Define `type Action uint8` with constants: ActionNone=0, ActionPrint=1, ActionExecute=2, ActionCSIDispatch=3, ActionEscDispatch=4, ActionOSCEnd=5, ActionDCSEnd=6. Define `type Parser struct` with fields: state State, paramBuf []byte (capacity 128), intermBuf []byte, dcsLen int, maxDCSLen int (default 4096). Add NewParser() *Parser.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: NewParser() returns a parser in StateGround with empty buffers and maxDCSLen=4096. All state and action constants have correct values. Test in vt/parser_test.go.

sequentialTasks.task: Implement table-driven ANSI parser state transitions in vt/parser.go
sequentialTasks.description: Add func (p *Parser) Feed(b byte) (Action, byte) to vt/parser.go. This is a table-driven replacement for processByte/processGround/processEscape/processCSI/processOSC/processDCS from vterm.go. Rather than switch/case nesting, define a transition table: [stateCount][256]struct{action Action; nextState State}. Feed() looks up the table entry for (currentState, inputByte), performs the action, transitions state, and returns (action, finalByte). For CSI: accumulate param bytes (0x30-0x3F) and intermediate bytes (0x20-0x2F) into buffers. For ground state: bytes 0x20-0x7F return ActionPrint. Control chars (0x00-0x1F) in ground return ActionExecute. ESC transitions to StateEscape. This replaces the ad-hoc state machine in vterm.go.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test in vt/parser_test.go: Feed ESC byte transitions to StateEscape. Feed '[' after ESC transitions to StateCSI. CSI parameter bytes accumulate. CSI final byte (0x40-0x7E) returns ActionCSIDispatch with the final byte. OSC terminated by BEL returns ActionOSCEnd. DCS terminated by ESC+backslash returns. Printable ASCII in ground returns ActionPrint. Control chars return ActionExecute. Table lookup is O(1) per byte.

sequentialTasks.task: Add parser parameter extraction in vt/parser.go
sequentialTasks.description: Add func (p *Parser) Params() []int to vt/parser.go. Parses p.paramBuf as semicolon-separated decimal integers. Empty string returns empty slice. Missing params return 0 (e.g., ';5' → [0, 5]). This replaces parseParams from vterm.go lines ~715-745. Add func (p *Parser) Intermediates() []byte returning a copy of p.intermBuf. Add func (p *Parser) HasIntermediate(b byte) bool checking if an intermediate byte is present (replaces the isPrivate checks in dispatchCSI). Add func (p *Parser) Reset() clearing state back to StateGround.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test in vt/parser_test.go: Params() on '1;2;3' returns [1,2,3]. Params() on '' returns []. Params() on '5' returns [5]. Params() on ';5' returns [0,5]. Params() on '1;;3' returns [1,0,3]. HasIntermediate('?') after CSI ? sequence returns true. Reset() puts parser back in StateGround with empty buffers.

sequentialTasks.task: Implement UTF-8 byte accumulation in vt/utf8.go
sequentialTasks.description: Create internal/termmux/vt/utf8.go. Define `type UTF8Accum struct` with fields: buf [utf8.UTFMax]byte, len int. Add func (u *UTF8Accum) Feed(b byte) (rune, bool). Feed processes one byte: if byte starts a new multi-byte sequence (>= 0x80), begin accumulating. If byte is a continuation byte (0x80-0xBF), append to buffer. When utf8.FullRune, decode and return (rune, true). On invalid continuation (byte < 0x80 or >= 0xC0 when accumulating), return (utf8.RuneError, true) and do NOT consume the byte (caller must re-feed). Add func (u *UTF8Accum) Pending() bool returning whether a partial sequence is buffered. Add func (u *UTF8Accum) Flush() rune returning RuneError if bytes remain, clearing the buffer.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test in vt/utf8_test.go: Single ASCII byte returns immediately. 2-byte UTF-8 (é = 0xC3 0xA9) accumulates then returns. 3-byte UTF-8 (漢 = 0xE6 0xBC 0xA2) accumulates then returns. 4-byte UTF-8 (𝕊 = 0xF0 0x9D 0x95 0x8A) accumulates then returns. Truncated sequence followed by ASCII emits RuneError then the ASCII is re-feedable. Overlong sequences emit RuneError. Flush on partial returns RuneError. Pending() is true during accumulation.

sequentialTasks.task: Implement rune width cache in vt/utf8.go
sequentialTasks.description: Add a width cache to vt/utf8.go. Define `type WidthCache struct { cache map[rune]int }`. Add func NewWidthCache() *WidthCache. Add func (w *WidthCache) Width(r rune) int that caches uniseg.StringWidth(string(r)) lookups. The cache is not shared across goroutines (VTerm has its own). This is a performance optimization — uniseg.StringWidth involves Unicode table lookups on every call. Use github.com/rivo/uniseg ONLY (go-runewidth is BANNED).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test in vt/utf8_test.go: Width('A')=1, Width('漢')=2, Width(0)=0. Second call for same rune returns from cache (verify via benchmark showing cache is faster than uncached). Width of control chars returns 0 per uniseg behavior.

sequentialTasks.task: Implement CSI sequence dispatch in vt/csi.go
sequentialTasks.description: Create internal/termmux/vt/csi.go. Define func DispatchCSI(scr *Screen, final byte, params []int, isPrivate bool). This is a table-driven replacement for dispatchCSI in vterm.go lines ~530-710. Create a dispatch table: map[byte]func(*Screen, []int, bool). Register handlers for: A=CUU, B=CUD, C=CUF, D=CUB, E=CNL, F=CPL, G=CHA, H/f=CUP/HVP, I=CHT, J=ED, K=EL, L=IL, M=DL, P=DCH, X=ECH, Z=CBT, @=ICH, S=SU, T=SD, d=VPA, g=TBC, m=SGR, r=DECSTBM, h=DECSET, l=DECRST, s=SCP, u=RCP. Add paramDefault(params []int, idx, def int) int helper.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test in vt/csi_test.go: Each CSI sequence modifies the Screen correctly. CUP moves cursor. ED erases display. SGR changes attributes. DECSTBM sets scroll region and homes cursor. DECSET/DECRST toggle cursor visibility and alt screen. Unknown final bytes are silently ignored. Table-driven dispatch has no switch/case on final byte.

sequentialTasks.task: Implement DECSET/DECRST handler in vt/csi.go
sequentialTasks.description: Add handleDECSET(scr *Screen, params []int) and handleDECRST(scr *Screen, params []int) to vt/csi.go. These replace handleDECSET/handleDECRST from vterm.go. Handle mode 25 (DECTCEM: cursor visibility — set CursorVisible). Handle mode 1049 (alt screen buffer — swap to alternate/primary screen). Handle mode 47 and 1047 as aliases for 1049. For alt screen switch, define a callback mechanism: DispatchCSI takes an optional AltScreenSwitch func(toAlt bool) so the parent VTerm can manage primary/alternate buffer swapping.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test in vt/csi_test.go: CSI ?25l hides cursor, CSI ?25h shows cursor. CSI ?1049h triggers alt screen callback with toAlt=true. CSI ?1049l triggers callback with toAlt=false. Modes 47 and 1047 also trigger the callback. Unknown private modes are silently ignored.

sequentialTasks.task: Implement ESC sequence dispatch in vt/esc.go
sequentialTasks.description: Create internal/termmux/vt/esc.go. Define func DispatchESC(scr *Screen, final byte) with optional callback for sequences that need VTerm-level state (RIS=full reset, alt screen). Handle: 7=DECSC (save cursor+attr), 8=DECRC (restore cursor+attr), M=RI (reverse index), D=IND (index/line feed), E=NEL (next line), c=RIS (reset — callback), H=HTS (set tab stop). Unknown escape bytes are silently ignored.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test in vt/esc_test.go: DECSC saves cursor position and attr, DECRC restores them. RI at top of screen scrolls down. IND at bottom scrolls up. NEL moves to column 0 of next line. HTS sets a tab stop at current column. RIS triggers reset callback.

sequentialTasks.task: Implement OSC sequence consumption in vt/osc.go
sequentialTasks.description: Create internal/termmux/vt/osc.go. Define func ConsumeOSC(p *Parser, b byte) bool — returns true when the OSC is terminated (BEL or ST). OSC sequences are consumed and discarded (we don't need window title, etc.). The parser already handles the state transition; this function exists to document the behavior and provide a hook for future OSC handling if needed.

sequentialTasks.task: Implement DCS sequence consumption in vt/osc.go
sequentialTasks.description: Add DCS consumption to vt/osc.go (or a separate vt/dcs.go if cleaner). DCS sequences are consumed and discarded like OSC. Enforce a max payload length (4096 bytes, matching maxDCSLen from vterm.go line 483). If payload exceeds max, abort to ground state. The parser handles state; this provides the length guard.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test in vt/osc_test.go (or vt/dcs_test.go): DCS terminated by ST is consumed. DCS terminated by BEL is consumed. DCS exceeding 4096 bytes aborts to ground state. Parser state is clean after termination.

sequentialTasks.task: Implement VTerm composite type in vt/vt.go
sequentialTasks.description: Create internal/termmux/vt/vt.go. Define `type VTerm struct` composing: primary *Screen, alternate *Screen, active *Screen (pointer to primary or alternate), parser *Parser, utf8 UTF8Accum, widthCache *WidthCache, rows/cols int, mu sync.Mutex. Add NewVTerm(rows, cols int) *VTerm. Add Resize(rows, cols int). Add Rows()/Cols() int accessors. The VTerm owns the concurrency lock; Screen/Parser are not themselves thread-safe.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test in vt/vt_test.go: NewVTerm(24,80) has 24 rows, 80 cols, primary==active, parser in ground state. Resize(12,40) updates rows/cols and resizes both screens. Rows() and Cols() return current dimensions. Concurrent Write() and Resize() do not race (run with -race).

sequentialTasks.task: Implement VTerm.Write() byte processing loop in vt/vt.go
sequentialTasks.description: Add Write(p []byte) (int, error) to VTerm implementing io.Writer. Port the logic from internal/termui/mux/vterm.go Write() at lines 271-340. For each byte: (1) if UTF-8 accumulation is active, feed to utf8 accumulator — on complete rune, call active.PutChar(r). (2) If ground state and byte >= 0x80, start UTF-8 accumulation. (3) Otherwise feed to parser: check returned Action — ActionPrint calls PutChar, ActionExecute handles control chars (LF calls LineFeed, CR resets col, TAB advances to tab stop, BS decrements col, BEL ignored), ActionCSIDispatch calls DispatchCSI, ActionEscDispatch calls DispatchESC. (4) Always return len(p), nil.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test in vt/vt_test.go: Writing 'Hello' places 5 ASCII chars. Writing 'Hello\nWorld' places on two lines. Writing CSI sequence '\x1b[2J' clears display. Writing UTF-8 multi-byte char places correctly. Writing bytes split across two Write calls (e.g., first byte of UTF-8 in call 1, remaining in call 2) reassembles correctly. All this verifiable by inspecting active screen's Cells.

sequentialTasks.task: Implement control character dispatch in VTerm.Write()
sequentialTasks.description: Within the Write() method's ActionExecute path, handle all control characters: 0x07=BEL (ignore), 0x08=BS (decrement CurCol if >0, clear PendingWrap), 0x09=TAB (advance to next tab stop, clear PendingWrap), 0x0A=LF (clear PendingWrap, call LineFeed), 0x0D=CR (clear PendingWrap, set CurCol=0). All other control chars (0x00-0x06, 0x0B-0x0C, 0x0E-0x1A, 0x1C-0x1F) are silently ignored. This matches processGround from vterm.go.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test in vt/vt_test.go: Tab advances to next 8-column stop. Backspace decrements column. CR returns to column 0. LF advances line. BEL does nothing. Null byte does nothing. Vertical tab (0x0B) does nothing.

sequentialTasks.task: Implement alt screen buffer switching in VTerm
sequentialTasks.description: Add methods to VTerm: SwitchToAlt() and SwitchToPrimary(). SwitchToAlt sets active=alternate. SwitchToPrimary sets active=primary. Wire into the DECSET/DECRST callback mechanism from T022: when CSI ?1049h is dispatched, VTerm.SwitchToAlt() is called; when CSI ?1049l, VTerm.SwitchToPrimary(). Also handle 1049's save/restore cursor behavior: 1049h saves cursor on primary before switch, 1049l restores cursor on primary after switch.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test in vt/vt_test.go: Write text, switch to alt, write different text — alt screen has second text, primary preserved. Switch back — primary text restored. Cursor position is saved/restored across 1049h/1049l. Both mode 47 and 1047 also work.

sequentialTasks.task: Implement RIS (full reset) in VTerm
sequentialTasks.description: Add Reset() method to VTerm. Called when ESC c is dispatched. Creates fresh primary and alternate screens (NewScreen), sets active=primary, resets parser to ground state, clears UTF-8 accumulator. Wire into DispatchESC callback for 'c'.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test in vt/vt_test.go: Write colored text, set alt screen, move cursor — then Write ESC c. After reset: primary is blank, alternate is blank, active is primary, cursor at 0,0, attrs are default.

sequentialTasks.task: Implement screen rendering in vt/render.go
sequentialTasks.description: Create internal/termmux/vt/render.go. Define func Render(scr *Screen) string. Port the Render() logic from vterm.go lines 1202-1262. For each row: find last non-default cell. Skip entirely blank rows. Emit CUP to position cursor at row start. For each cell up to last non-default: skip zero-width placeholders (Ch==0). Emit SGRDiff(prevAttr, cell.Attr) on attr change. Emit the rune. After all rows: emit \x1b[0m reset, emit CUP to cursor position, emit cursor visibility (\x1b[?25h or \x1b[?25l).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test in vt/render_test.go: Empty 3×5 screen renders only the reset+cursor position. Screen with 'AB' at row 0 renders CUP(1,1)+A+B+reset+cursor. Bold text includes SGR sequence. Wide character renders correctly (no double-rendering). Render is idempotent: Render(same_screen) produces same output.

sequentialTasks.task: Add Render() method to VTerm delegating to vt.Render()
sequentialTasks.description: Add func (v *VTerm) Render() string to vt/vt.go. Acquires v.mu, calls Render(v.active), returns the result. This is the public API for screen restoration.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test in vt/vt_test.go: Write known ANSI output to VTerm, call Render(), verify the output contains expected CUP sequences and character content. Render() under concurrent Write() does not race.

sequentialTasks.task: Add dirty tracking to Screen for incremental rendering
sequentialTasks.description: Add a dirty tracking mechanism to Screen in vt/screen.go. Add field `dirty []bool` (one per row, true if row changed since last render). Set dirty[row]=true in PutChar, all erase operations, all scroll operations, all line insert/delete operations. Add func (s *Screen) DirtyRows() []int returning indices of dirty rows. Add func (s *Screen) ClearDirty(). Add func RenderDirty(scr *Screen) string in vt/render.go that only renders dirty rows (optimization for status bar updates and partial redraws).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test in vt/screen_test.go: NewScreen has no dirty rows. PutChar marks current row dirty. EraseDisplay marks all rows dirty. ClearDirty resets. ScrollUp marks scroll region rows dirty. Test in vt/render_test.go: RenderDirty produces output only for changed rows.

sequentialTasks.task: Add String() debug dump to VTerm
sequentialTasks.description: Add func (v *VTerm) String() string to vt/vt.go. Returns a plain-text representation of the active screen (no ANSI, just characters) for debugging and test assertions. Each row is a string of runes, trailing spaces stripped, rows joined by newline. This is simpler than Render() and useful for test assertions. Also add func (v *VTerm) CursorPos() (row, col int) accessor.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test in vt/vt_test.go: Write 'Hello\nWorld', String() returns 'Hello\nWorld'. Write '\x1b[2J', String() returns empty-ish (just newlines or empty string). CursorPos() returns expected position after writes.

sequentialTasks.task: Create ptyio interfaces and types in ptyio/ptyio.go
sequentialTasks.description: Create internal/termmux/ptyio/ptyio.go. Define interfaces: `type PTY interface { io.ReadWriteCloser; Fd() uintptr; Resize(rows, cols uint16) error }`. Define `type BlockingGuard interface { EnsureBlocking(fd int) (origFlags int, err error); Restore(fd int, origFlags int) }`. Define `type TermState interface { MakeRaw(fd int) (*term.State, error); Restore(fd int, state *term.State) error; GetSize(fd int) (width, height int, err error) }`. These interfaces abstract platform-specific operations for testability.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Interfaces compile. They match the operations used in mux.go RunPassthrough: MakeRaw, Restore, GetSize, EnsureBlocking. A mock implementation for tests can be written implementing these interfaces.

sequentialTasks.task: Implement real TermState adapter in ptyio/ptyio.go
sequentialTasks.description: Add `type RealTermState struct{}` implementing TermState. MakeRaw delegates to golang.org/x/term.MakeRaw. Restore delegates to term.Restore. GetSize delegates to term.GetSize. This is the production implementation; tests use a mock.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: RealTermState{} satisfies the TermState interface (compile-time check: var _ TermState = RealTermState{}). These methods are thin wrappers with no logic to test beyond the compile check.

sequentialTasks.task: Implement blocking guard for Unix in ptyio/blocking_unix.go
sequentialTasks.description: Create internal/termmux/ptyio/blocking_unix.go with build tag `//go:build !windows`. Define `type UnixBlockingGuard struct{}` implementing BlockingGuard. EnsureBlocking uses fcntl F_GETFL/F_SETFL to clear O_NONBLOCK (port from mux_blocking_unix.go). Restore re-sets the original flags. Extract the fcntlGetFlags/fcntlSetFlags helpers from mux_blocking_unix.go.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Compile-time interface check passes on Unix. The fcntl operations are the same as the existing code. Unit test in ptyio/blocking_unix_test.go (build-tagged) creates a pipe, sets O_NONBLOCK, calls EnsureBlocking, verifies O_NONBLOCK is cleared, calls Restore, verifies O_NONBLOCK is restored.

sequentialTasks.task: Implement blocking guard no-op for Windows in ptyio/blocking_windows.go
sequentialTasks.description: Create internal/termmux/ptyio/blocking_windows.go with build tag `//go:build windows`. Define `type WindowsBlockingGuard struct{}` implementing BlockingGuard. Both methods are no-ops (EnsureBlocking returns 0, nil; Restore returns immediately). Port from mux_blocking_other.go.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Compile-time interface check passes on Windows. Both methods do nothing and return no error.

sequentialTasks.task: Implement buffered PTY reader in ptyio/reader.go
sequentialTasks.description: Create internal/termmux/ptyio/reader.go. Define `type BufferedReader struct` wrapping an io.Reader with a configurable buffer size (default 32KB), a channel-based output mechanism, and backpressure awareness. ReadLoop(ctx context.Context) runs the blocking read loop, sending []byte chunks on an output channel. If the output channel is full (consumer paused), the reader blocks — providing backpressure to the child process. Add a Done() <-chan struct{} for lifecycle tracking. This replaces the ad-hoc backgroundReader goroutine in mux.go lines 235-275 with a structured, testable component.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test in ptyio/reader_test.go: (1) Reader reads from a pipe, sends chunks on output channel. (2) Closing the pipe causes Done() to close. (3) When output channel is full, reader blocks (backpressure). (4) Context cancellation stops the reader. (5) Run with -race, no races. (6) After Done(), no goroutine leak (use runtime.NumGoroutine before/after).

sequentialTasks.task: Implement buffered PTY writer with retry logic in ptyio/writer.go
sequentialTasks.description: Create internal/termmux/ptyio/writer.go. Define `type BufferedWriter struct` wrapping an io.Writer. Implement Write(p []byte) (int, error) that handles partial writes: if the underlying Write returns n < len(p) and err==nil, retry with remaining bytes. If err is EAGAIN/EWOULDBLOCK, retry after runtime.Gosched(). Track total bytes written. This replaces the unsafe child.Write(buf[:n]) in mux.go's stdin forwarding that assumes atomic writes.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test in ptyio/writer_test.go: (1) Normal write passes through. (2) Writer that returns partial write (n < len) triggers retry for remaining bytes. (3) Writer that returns EAGAIN triggers retry. (4) Writer that returns a real error propagates it. (5) Total bytes written is correct after partial retries.

sequentialTasks.task: Implement PTY resize for Unix in ptyio/resize_unix.go
sequentialTasks.description: Create internal/termmux/ptyio/resize_unix.go with build tag `//go:build !windows`. Define func ResizePTY(fd uintptr, rows, cols uint16) error using TIOCSWINSZ ioctl (syscall.Syscall with SYS_IOCTL). Define the winsize struct matching the C struct. This is not currently in the old code (it used the resizeFn callback), but a native implementation is needed for the ptyio package to be self-contained.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test in ptyio/resize_unix_test.go: Create a real PTY pair (os/exec + pty library or creack/pty), call ResizePTY, verify the child sees the new size (or at minimum, the ioctl doesn't return an error). If no PTY library is available, test that it compiles and the ioctl call structure is correct.

sequentialTasks.task: Implement PTY resize for Windows in ptyio/resize_windows.go
sequentialTasks.description: Create internal/termmux/ptyio/resize_windows.go with build tag `//go:build windows`. Define func ResizePTY(fd uintptr, rows, cols uint16) error. On Windows, ConPTY resize uses ResizePseudoConsole. For now, implement as a no-op that returns nil (Windows PTY support can be refined later). Document the TODO.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Compiles on Windows. Returns nil. Has a TODO comment referencing ResizePseudoConsole for future implementation.

sequentialTasks.task: Implement status bar renderer in statusbar/statusbar.go
sequentialTasks.description: Create internal/termmux/statusbar/statusbar.go. Define `type StatusBar struct` with fields: status string, toggleKeyName string, writer io.Writer, height int (total terminal height), mu sync.Mutex. Add New(w io.Writer) *StatusBar. Add SetHeight(h int). Add SetStatus(s string). Add SetToggleKey(key byte) (converts to readable name like 'Ctrl+]'). Add Render() that writes the status bar to the last terminal row: save cursor, move to row=height, clear line, write reverse-video '[Claude] status │ Ctrl+] to switch', restore cursor. Port from renderStatusBar in mux.go lines 413-420.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test in statusbar/statusbar_test.go: Render to a bytes.Buffer. Verify output contains save cursor (ESC 7 or CSI s), CUP to last row, clear line (CSI 2K), reverse video (SGR 7), status text, toggle key hint, restore cursor. SetStatus changes the rendered text. SetToggleKey(0x1D) renders 'Ctrl+]'. SetToggleKey(0x01) renders 'Ctrl+A'.

sequentialTasks.task: Implement scroll region management in statusbar/statusbar.go
sequentialTasks.description: Add SetScrollRegion() and ResetScrollRegion() methods to StatusBar. SetScrollRegion writes CSI 1;(height-1)r CSI 1;1H — restricting scrolling to rows 1..height-1, reserving the last row for the status bar. ResetScrollRegion writes CSI r CSI 999;1H — restoring full scroll and positioning cursor at bottom. Port from setScrollRegion/resetScrollRegion in mux.go lines 401-410.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test in statusbar/statusbar_test.go: SetScrollRegion writes the expected ANSI sequences. ResetScrollRegion writes the expected ANSI sequences. Verify exact byte sequences match the old implementation. Setting height=24 and calling SetScrollRegion produces CSI 1;23r.

sequentialTasks.task: Define Mux public API type in termmux/termmux.go
sequentialTasks.description: Create internal/termmux/termmux.go. Define `type Mux struct` with fields: cfg Config, stdin io.Reader, stdout io.Writer, termFd int, mu sync.Mutex, child io.ReadWriteCloser, active Side, passthroughActive bool, swappedOnce bool, vterm *vt.VTerm, termRows/termCols int, reader *ptyio.BufferedReader, statusBar *statusbar.StatusBar, termState ptyio.TermState, blockingGuard ptyio.BlockingGuard. Add `func New(stdin io.Reader, stdout io.Writer, termFd int, opts ...Option) *Mux`. Apply defaults, create StatusBar, assign platform-appropriate BlockingGuard and TermState.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: New() creates a Mux with default config. ToggleKey is 0x1D. StatusEnabled is true. Active side is SideOsm. Child is nil. VTerm is nil. Test in termmux/termmux_test.go.

sequentialTasks.task: Implement Mux.Attach() in termmux/termmux.go
sequentialTasks.description: Add Attach(child io.ReadWriteCloser) error to Mux. Port from mux.go Attach() lines 157-188. If child already attached, return ErrAlreadyAttached. Set m.child. Create VTerm with current termRows/termCols (default 24×80). Start BufferedReader goroutine reading from child. The reader's output channel is consumed by a tee goroutine that writes to VTerm always and to stdout only when passthroughActive.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test in termmux/termmux_test.go: Attach succeeds. Double Attach returns ErrAlreadyAttached. After Attach, HasChild() returns true. VTerm is allocated. BufferedReader is started.

sequentialTasks.task: Implement Mux tee goroutine for child output routing
sequentialTasks.description: Internal to Attach(): start a goroutine that reads from BufferedReader's output channel and (1) always writes to VTerm for screen capture, (2) writes to stdout only when m.passthroughActive is true. This replaces backgroundReader in mux.go lines 235-275. The goroutine exits when the reader's Done() channel closes. Use a separate done channel (m.teeDone) for Detach() to wait on.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test in termmux/termmux_test.go: Attach a mock child that writes 'Hello'. In non-passthrough mode, VTerm captures 'Hello' but stdout does NOT receive it. Set passthroughActive=true, child writes 'World' — stdout receives 'World' AND VTerm captures it. Run with -race.

sequentialTasks.task: Implement Mux.Detach() in termmux/termmux.go
sequentialTasks.description: Add Detach() error to Mux. Port from mux.go Detach() lines 190-225. If passthroughActive, return ErrPassthroughActive. Nil out child and VTerm. Wait for tee goroutine to finish (with timeout). Reset swappedOnce. The key difference from old code: BufferedReader has a proper Done() channel, so we wait on that instead of a bare channel with a 3-second timeout. Use a configurable detach timeout (default 5s) instead of hardcoded 3s.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test in termmux/termmux_test.go: Detach after Attach succeeds. HasChild() returns false after Detach. Detach during passthrough returns ErrPassthroughActive. Detach with child already closed returns immediately (no timeout). Detach with child still open waits up to timeout. No goroutine leak after Detach (verify with runtime.NumGoroutine).

sequentialTasks.task: Implement Mux accessor methods
sequentialTasks.description: Add to termmux.go: ActiveSide() Side, HasChild() bool, SetToggleKey(byte), SetResizeFunc(fn), SetStatusEnabled(bool), SetClaudeStatus(string), WriteToChild([]byte)(int,error). Direct ports from mux.go lines 278-345. All are mutex-guarded.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test in termmux/termmux_test.go: Each accessor sets/gets the expected value. WriteToChild with no child returns ErrNoChild. WriteToChild with child delegates to child.Write(). SetClaudeStatus updates the statusBar.

sequentialTasks.task: Implement RunPassthrough terminal state save/restore
sequentialTasks.description: Begin implementing RunPassthrough(ctx context.Context) (ExitReason, error) in termmux.go. First section: precondition checks (no child → ErrNoChild, already active → ErrPassthroughActive). Set passthroughActive=true, active=SideClaude. Save terminal state via m.termState.MakeRaw(). Ensure blocking via m.blockingGuard.EnsureBlocking(). Set up defer to restore: term state, blocking flags, passthroughActive=false, active=SideOsm. Port from mux.go RunPassthrough lines 350-400.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test in termmux/termmux_test.go with mock TermState and BlockingGuard: RunPassthrough calls MakeRaw, EnsureBlocking on entry. After return (via toggle key), Restore and blocking Restore are called. ActiveSide is SideClaude during passthrough and SideOsm after.

sequentialTasks.task: Implement RunPassthrough status bar setup
sequentialTasks.description: In RunPassthrough, after terminal state save: if StatusEnabled, get terminal size, set status bar height, call statusBar.SetScrollRegion(), statusBar.Render(). Defer statusBar.ResetScrollRegion(). Calculate childRows = height - statusBarLines for VTerm sizing. Resize VTerm to childRows×width. Update termRows/termCols. Port from mux.go RunPassthrough lines 400-440.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test in termmux/termmux_test.go: When StatusEnabled=true and mock termState returns 80×24, scroll region is set to 1..23. StatusBar is rendered. VTerm is resized to 23×80. When StatusEnabled=false, no scroll region is set.

sequentialTasks.task: Implement RunPassthrough first-swap screen clear
sequentialTasks.description: In RunPassthrough, after status bar setup: if this is the first passthrough (!swappedOnce), clear screen (ESC[2J ESC[H), call resizeFn to propagate dimensions to child PTY. Set swappedOnce=true. Port from mux.go lines 440-460.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test in termmux/termmux_test.go: First call to RunPassthrough (after Attach) writes ESC[2J ESC[H to stdout and calls resizeFn with correct dimensions. Second call does NOT clear screen.

sequentialTasks.task: Implement RunPassthrough VTerm screen restore on toggle-back
sequentialTasks.description: In RunPassthrough, if NOT first swap: restore Claude's screen from VTerm.Render(). Write ESC[2J ESC[H to clear, then write rendered output. Re-render status bar after VTerm restore. Port from mux.go lines 462-485. The key fix vs old code: hold m.mu during the clear+render+statusbar sequence to prevent interleaving with tee goroutine writes.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test in termmux/termmux_test.go: Attach child that writes colored output. First RunPassthrough (clear screen). Toggle out. Second RunPassthrough restores VTerm content — stdout receives the Render() output. Status bar is rendered after VTerm content. No interleaving of tee writes during restore (verify with a slow mock child writing during restore).

sequentialTasks.task: Implement RunPassthrough stdin forwarding with toggle key detection
sequentialTasks.description: In RunPassthrough, start stdin→child goroutine. Read from m.stdin, scan for toggleKey. If found: forward bytes before the toggle key, send ExitToggle on result channel. Otherwise, forward all bytes to child via BufferedWriter. Handle EAGAIN with retry (defense-in-depth even after EnsureBlocking). Port from mux.go lines 500-545. Key difference: use ptyio.BufferedWriter for child writes instead of raw child.Write.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test in termmux/termmux_test.go: Write normal bytes to stdin — child receives them. Write toggle key — RunPassthrough returns ExitToggle. Write 'abc' + toggleKey + 'def' — child receives 'abc', RunPassthrough returns ExitToggle, 'def' is not forwarded. EAGAIN on stdin.Read triggers retry, not error exit.

sequentialTasks.task: Implement RunPassthrough child exit and context cancellation handling
sequentialTasks.description: In RunPassthrough, after starting stdin goroutine: select on result channel (from stdin goroutine), BufferedReader's Done channel (child EOF), and ctx.Done(). Child EOF → ExitChildExit. Context cancel → ExitContext. Port from mux.go lines 547-560. Cancel the forwarding context on exit to clean up the stdin goroutine.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test in termmux/termmux_test.go: Close child PTY during passthrough → returns ExitChildExit. Cancel context during passthrough → returns ExitContext. Stdin goroutine is cleaned up after each exit (no goroutine leak).

sequentialTasks.task: Implement StringIO adapter in termmux/stringio.go
sequentialTasks.description: Create internal/termmux/stringio.go. Port the StringIO interface and WrapStringIO adapter from mux.go lines 424-end. StringIO has Send(string) error, Receive() (string, error), Close() error. WrapStringIO returns an io.ReadWriteCloser that adapts string-based I/O (used by claudemux AgentHandle) to byte-based I/O (used by Mux.Attach). The Read side calls Receive() and buffers the result. The Write side calls Send().
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test in termmux/stringio_test.go: Port and adapt TestWrapStringIO_ReadWrite from mux_test.go. Verify: Write('hello') calls Send('hello'). Read fills buffer from Receive(). EOF from Receive propagates as io.EOF. Close calls underlying Close. Concurrent Read/Write is safe.

sequentialTasks.task: Implement Mux.SetResizeFunc re-render on SIGWINCH
sequentialTasks.description: In termmux/termmux.go, when the terminal is resized during passthrough (SIGWINCH), the Mux should: update termRows/termCols, resize VTerm, call resizeFn to propagate to child, re-render status bar. Add handleResize(rows, cols int) method. For SIGWINCH detection, use signal.Notify in RunPassthrough (Unix only). On Windows, this is a no-op.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Test in termmux/termmux_test.go: Simulate resize by calling handleResize directly. Verify VTerm is resized, resizeFn is called, status bar is re-rendered with new height. Integration test with real terminal resize (if possible) or manual verification.

sequentialTasks.task: Add SIGWINCH handling for Unix in termmux/resize_unix.go
sequentialTasks.description: Create internal/termmux/resize_unix.go with build tag `//go:build !windows`. Implement func watchResize(ctx context.Context, termFd int, fn func(rows, cols int)) that sets up signal.Notify for syscall.SIGWINCH. When received, gets terminal size via term.GetSize and calls fn. Exits cleanly when ctx is cancelled. Create internal/termmux/resize_windows.go as no-op.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: watchResize exits when context is cancelled (no goroutine leak). On SIGWINCH, fn is called with current terminal dimensions. Build-tagged correctly for Unix/Windows.

sequentialTasks.task: Wire SIGWINCH handler into RunPassthrough
sequentialTasks.description: In termmux/termmux.go RunPassthrough, after terminal state setup, start watchResize goroutine. On resize callback: re-acquire terminal size, resize VTerm to (newRows - statusBarLines) × newCols, call resizeFn, re-render status bar. Cancel the resize watcher on RunPassthrough exit.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: During passthrough, terminal resize triggers VTerm resize, child PTY resize (via resizeFn), and status bar re-render. After RunPassthrough exits, the resize watcher goroutine is cleaned up.
sequentialTasks.status: Done (watchResize goroutine started in RunPassthrough with resizeCtx, cancelled on exit)

sequentialTasks.task: Write test: VTerm handles interleaved CSI and UTF-8
sequentialTasks.description: In vt/vt_test.go: Write a sequence that mixes CSI cursor movement with UTF-8 characters: '\x1b[5;10H漢字\x1b[1;1HABC'. Verify '漢字' appears at row 5, col 10 (occupying cols 10-13) and 'ABC' at row 1, col 1. This tests that the parser correctly distinguishes between UTF-8 lead bytes (>= 0x80) and escape sequences, even when interleaved.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Mixed CSI + UTF-8 output produces correct screen state. UTF-8 bytes are not misinterpreted as CSI parameters. CSI sequences are not corrupted by adjacent UTF-8 bytes.
sequentialTasks.status: Done (漢字 at row 5 col 10, ABC at row 1 col 1 — all verified)

continuousVerification.description: After each code change, test addition, or bug fix, run `make 2>&1 | tail -50` to verify the full pipeline (build + lint + test). For faster iteration on a single package, use `go test -race ./internal/termmux/vt/...` but ALWAYS run full `make` before marking a task Done.
continuousVerification.failPolicy: If ANY test fails, STOP IMMEDIATELY and fix the issue. Zero tolerance for test failures, race conditions, or 'works on my machine' excuses. Fix the test or fix the code — never skip.
continuousVerification.testCoverage: Aim for >90% coverage in vt/, ptyio/, statusbar/ packages. Verify with `go test -coverprofile=cover.out ./internal/termmux/... && go tool cover -func=cover.out | tail -20`.

finalEnforcementProtocol.alert: NEVER add entries to .deadcodeignore. If deadcode flags termmux functions, they must be wired into the call graph from cmd/osm or deleted.
ALL tests must pass on ALL platforms: ubuntu-latest, windows-latest, macos-latest. Build-tag platform-specific code. Never accept 'it passes on my machine' as sufficient.
Pipe long output through tail/head to prevent context flooding. `make test 2>&1 | tail -50`.
The old internal/termui/mux/ must be DELETED (T100), not left as dead code. Verify with grep that no imports reference it.
